light-background.usage: Primary light canvas. Keep it softly tinted; never replace it with pure white.

light-foreground.usage: Primary text in light mode, with a warm technical tint.

dark-background.usage: Primary dark desktop canvas and default app-shell ground.

dark-foreground.usage: Primary text in dark mode, warm enough to avoid sterile blue-gray.

primary.usage: Primary action, selected state, active navigation, rings, and chart emphasis. Use sparingly.

focus-border.usage: Keyboard focus borders, resize-handle focus bars, and strong desktop focus fills.

destructive.usage: Destructive actions, invalid states, auth errors, and repository operation failures.

display.purpose: Rare onboarding and empty-state moments where there is no competing repository data.

headline.purpose: Settings titles and major pane titles that benefit from code-adjacent precision.

title.purpose: Dialogs, section headings, active repository labels, and toolbar groups.

body.purpose: Descriptions, helper copy, list metadata, and settings explanations.

label.purpose: Compact controls, uppercase group labels, shortcuts, badges, and table-like metadata.

elevation.philosophy: Flat by default. Depth comes from borders, tonal backgrounds, inset focus fills, subtle shadows, and pane structure.

subtle-surface.usage: Tiny separation for popovers, menus, and overlays in light mode.

dark-overlay.usage: Dropdowns and dialogs in dark mode.

motion.philosophy: Motion should acknowledge state changes without making repository work feel theatrical.
rules: Animate opacity, transform, and color only.
Do not animate layout properties in dense panes.

app-shell.structure: Header, scrollable main region, footer, and command palette branch selector.
rules: Keep chrome stable across repository routes.
Reserve onboarding for its own simplified shell.
Do not introduce marketing hero composition into app routes.

button.shape: Square corners, usually 28px high in operational surfaces.

states.hover: Muted tonal fill or foreground shift.
states.focus: Use desktop-focus or desktop-focus-strong inset treatment.

rules: Use primary only for the action that advances the current flow.
Keep labels available where user settings enable toolbar labels.

input.shape: Square corners with a clear 1px boundary.

states.focus: Inset focus fill and focus border.

pane.structure: Bordered, resizable, split, or scrollable regions for repository state.
rules: Prefer panes and splitters over standalone cards.
Use stable dimensions for tab bars, toolbars, trees, and diff regions.

resize-handle.shape: Thin vertical or horizontal control with focus bar pseudo-element.
rules: Do not rely on hover alone to communicate availability.

diff-workspace.structure: High-density decision surface for files, previews, blame, markdown, image comparison, terminal, and commit work.
rules: Keep file navigation and preview context close together.
Avoid layout shift when switching modes.
Reserve space for toolbars and state indicators.

desktop-focus.description: Inset 1px focus edge plus large inset tonal fill. Replaces generic ring treatment for desktop controls.

desktop-focus-strong.description: Stronger 2px inset focus edge for small icon buttons or high-risk focus targets.

desktop-focus-destructive.description: Risk Red focus treatment for destructive controls.

desktop-resize-handle-vertical-focus.description: Keyboard-visible vertical resize affordance with a centered 3px focus bar.

desktop-resize-handle-horizontal-focus.description: Keyboard-visible horizontal resize affordance with a centered 3px focus bar.

narrative.scene: A developer is reviewing repository state in a desktop app during focused work, with branch, diff, terminal, auth, and provider context close at hand.
antiReferences: cute language for destructive or remote-affecting Git actions

dos: Use existing OKLCH tokens from packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css and apps/desktop/src/styles/index.css.
Keep product screens compact, pane-based, and keyboard-friendly.
Use borders, low-opacity fills, and focus utilities for structure before adding shadows.
Keep risky Git and provider actions explicit, with clear labels and recovery paths.
Honor system theme, reduced motion, and platform-native behavior.
donts: Do not use glassmorphism, gradient text, neon-on-black styling, or big SaaS hero metrics inside product views.
Do not use colored border-left or border-right stripes as accents on cards, alerts, or list items.
Do not create repeated identical card grids for settings, actions, or repository state.
Do not make modals the default interaction for ordinary filtering, navigation, or progressive disclosure.
Do not use pure black or pure white; keep neutrals tinted through the existing OKLCH system.
